title: Aspidium veitchianum
intro: Aspidium veitchianum, commonly known as the Veitch's Lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Aspidium veitchianum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aspidium veitchianum is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications such as creams and ointments, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
taxonomy: Aspidium veitchianum is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody fern family. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships. More specifically, Aspidium veitchianum is placed within the genus Aspidium, which is now considered to be a synonym of the genus Dryopteris. However, some sources still recognize Aspidium as a distinct genus. Further research may be necessary to determine the most accurate classification.
